description_long: This object consists of two elongated, cylindrical orange carrot roots with slightly tapered ends and pale, rounded tops where the greens would have been attached. The surface is textured with subtle ridges and faint root hairs, giving it a natural, earthy appearance. The coloration is predominantly a vibrant orange, with the tops showing a pale yellowish hue. Both carrots are free of visible leaves, and their tips are pointed with some fine root filaments extending from the ends. The carrots are clean, with no visible dirt or soil attached to their surfaces. Their size and shape are typical of cultivated carrots found in urban markets or grocery stores.
description: Two clean, orange carrots with pointed tips and pale tops, showing natural surface texture and no attached leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing both carrots, one upright and one lying down, highlighting the pale tops and pointed tips.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the cylindrical shape and the fine root filaments at the tips of both carrots.
description_view_2: Angled view showing the length and tapering of the carrots, with clear visibility of the orange surface texture.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the upright carrot's pointed tip and the full length of the horizontal carrot.
